title: echinocereus engelmannii subsp. fasciculatus
intro: Echinocereus engelmannii subsp. fasciculatus, also known as Engelmann's hedgehog cactus, is a subspecies of the Echinocereus engelmannii plant, characterized by its distinctive clustering habit and vibrant green stems adorned with fine, sharp spines. The plant has been traditionally used in herbal medicine due to its medicinal properties, which include anti-inflammatory and antimicrobial properties, making it useful in treating wounds, infections, and other health issues. The health benefits of Echinocereus engelmannii subsp. fasciculatus include its potential to reduce inflammation, promote wound healing, and boost the immune system, as well as exhibit antioxidant and antiseptic properties. Herbal preparations of this plant can be made into teas, infusions, or salves, which can be used topically or internally to take advantage of its medicinal properties.
